name: global-commenting
description: Write minimal, evergreen code comments that explain complex logic without documenting obvious behavior or temporary changes. Use this skill when adding comments to PHP files, TypeScript/JavaScript files, or any code files, when documenting complex algorithms or business logic, when adding PHPDoc blocks or JSDoc comments, when writing self-documenting code with clear naming, or when reviewing existing comments for relevance and necessity. Focus on keeping code self-explanatory through clear structure and naming rather than relying heavily on comments.
capabilities: Provides guidance on writing minimal, meaningful comments
Helps document complex business logic and algorithms
Assists with PHPDoc and JSDoc comment formatting
Reviews existing comments for relevance and necessity
Promotes self-documenting code through clear naming
author: DevanB
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation files (SKILL.md and skill-report.json) with no executable code, network calls, or filesystem operations. All 17 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES caused by the scanner misidentifying SHA256 hashes as cryptographic algorithms and relative path references as path traversal. The skill is purely a documentation/prompt-based guidance system.
valueStatement: Code comments often become outdated or state the obvious. This skill helps you write meaningful, evergreen comments that explain complex logic and reduce maintenance burden.
userTitle: Write better code comments with AI guidance
limitations: Does not automatically generate comments for you
Requires human judgment for what needs documentation
Cannot analyze code complexity to suggest comments
Limited to guidance on comment style and best practices

useCases.title: Write better PHPDoc comments
useCases.description: Get guidance on documenting PHP classes, methods, and complex array shapes with proper PHPDoc syntax

useCases.title: Improve JSDoc documentation
useCases.description: Learn when and how to write meaningful JSDoc comments for functions and complex logic

useCases.title: Review comment quality
useCases.description: Evaluate existing comments for relevance, accuracy, and whether they should be refactored into clearer code

promptTemplates.title: Document complex algorithm
promptTemplates.scenario: When you have a complex sorting algorithm
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me write a comment for this sorting function that explains why we are using this specific algorithm instead of the built-in sort

promptTemplates.title: Add PHPDoc to class
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a new PHP service class
promptTemplates.prompt: Write PHPDoc comments for this service class including the purpose, main responsibilities, and any important implementation details

promptTemplates.title: Review comment necessity
promptTemplates.prompt: Review these comments and suggest which ones can be removed by improving the code clarity instead

promptTemplates.title: Document business rule
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me write a comment that explains this business rule and why it is implemented this way

bestPractices: Write comments that explain why not what - the code already shows what it does
Keep comments evergreen by avoiding references to temporary fixes or recent changes
Prefer refactoring code for clarity over adding explanatory comments
antiPatterns: Do not write obvious comments like increment counter - the code is already clear
Avoid commenting out code instead of deleting it - use version control for history
Do not use comments as an excuse for writing unclear code - fix the code first

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with all programming languages?
faq.answer: The principles apply universally, but examples focus on PHP, TypeScript, and JavaScript as used in Laravel projects.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically generate comments for my code?
faq.answer: No, it provides guidance and best practices. You still need to write the comments yourself based on the advice given.

faq.question: How does this integrate with my existing documentation tools?
faq.answer: It complements tools like PHPDoc and JSDoc by helping you write better content for your documentation blocks.

faq.question: Is my code sent to external services when using this skill?
faq.answer: No, this is a local skill that provides guidance. No code is transmitted to external services.

faq.question: What if I disagree with the commenting advice provided?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidelines, not rules. Use your judgment based on your team standards and project needs.

faq.question: How is this different from other documentation tools?
faq.answer: This focuses on the quality and necessity of comments rather than just formatting, helping you write meaningful documentation.
